one_line_verdict: All five R2 findings SURVIVE; the headline is a silent-alerting failure — 5 of 8 Prometheus alerts carry CamelCase metric names the lowercasing exporter never emits, so they look armed but never fire. Fix alerts.yml + harden the test before claiming the monitoring stack works.

surviving_findings.disposition: Lowercase the 4 CamelCase metric names in alerts.yml (cassandra_dropped_dropped, cassandra_compaction_pendingtasks, cassandra_client_request_latency_99thpercentile x2) to match jmx-exporter lowercaseOutputName:true, exactly as the Grafana dashboard already does.

Reconcile the hints alert (alerts.yml:59 cassandra_hints_TotalHintsInProgress) and dashboard (hcd-cluster.json:219 cassandra_hints_hints_created) onto the single lowercased series the exporter actually emits; the alert is the 5th silently-dead rule.

Strengthen test_prometheus_alerts_valid_yaml (test_scripts.py:104) to assert every metric name in each expr is all-lowercase (or resolves to a jmx-exporter-producible name); current test is shape-only and stays green over the R2-01/03 dead alerts.
Replace .gitignore:40 [hcd-node*] (dead POSIX char-class) with hcd-node*/ ; oracle git check-ignore confirms the rule is non-functional, though named-volume usage means low real impact.

Single-source the MinIO IP/image-tag across demo-entropy.sh (172.28.0.40 / 2024-11-07) and docker-compose.yml (172.28.0.10 / 2024-03-30); ALSO add MINIO_API_OBJECT_LOCK_ENABLED=on + --with-lock to the compose service so the make-minio path is not silently non-WORM (Defender-surfaced strength the Prosecutor missed).

lenses.sre: CONDITIONAL-SHIP. The cluster forms and the demo runs, but the monitoring layer is partly cosmetic: 5 of 8 alert rules (dropped messages, compaction backlog, write+read p99 latency, hints) will never fire because PromQL is case-sensitive and the exporter lowercases all names. The thing most likely to page someone at 3am is the alert that DOESN'T page — a real write-latency or dropped-message incident proceeds in silence while the dashboard (correctly lowercased) shows the pain nobody is alerted to. Block the merge of any 'monitoring is ready' claim until alerts.yml is lowercased.
lenses.committer: This round is config/test, not CQL/cassandra.yaml, so no live CQL statement would error. As a config reviewer I would NOT merge as-is: alerts.yml ships 5 provably non-matching metric names with a test too weak to catch them, and the author already demonstrated (in the dashboard) they know the names lowercase. Grade B-: the defect is mechanical, fully offline-detectable, and the fix is one sed plus one test assertion away from A.
lenses.security: conditions. No new auth/RBAC/CIDR/mTLS exposure introduced here. The one compliance-relevant gap is DORA/WORM: the docker-compose minio service (make minio) lacks MINIO_API_OBJECT_LOCK_ENABLED=on and --with-lock, so an operator who stands up MinIO via compose rather than the demo script gets a bucket with NO Object Lock — the WORM/immutability proof the ransomware modules assert does not hold on that path. Top obligation gap: make every documented MinIO path actually enforce Object Lock, or remove the non-WORM compose path so it cannot be mistaken for the audited one.

recommendations: Lowercase all 5 CamelCase metric names in config/alerts.yml (lines 29,39,59,69,79) to match jmx-exporter.yml lowercaseOutputName:true and the Grafana dashboard (R2-01 + R2-03).

Add a discriminating assertion to tests/test_scripts.py::test_prometheus_alerts_valid_yaml that every metric token in each expr is all-lowercase / jmx-exporter-producible, so this class of defect cannot regress green (R2-02).

Add MINIO_API_OBJECT_LOCK_ENABLED=on and the --with-lock server flag to the docker-compose minio service, and single-source its IP+image tag with demo-entropy.sh so make-minio and the demo agree and both enforce WORM (R2-05).
Fix .gitignore:40 to hcd-node*/ so the ignore rule is functional (R2-04).
unresolved: End-to-end firing of the corrected alerts cannot be confirmed offline — it needs a live cluster + Prometheus + JMX exporter to prove the lowercased names actually resolve to emitted series under load. The static evidence (lowercaseOutputName + dashboard parity) is strong but a live scrape is the final arbiter.
Whether the compose-path MinIO is ever used in practice (vs always the demo-script path) is a usage question the offline tribunal cannot settle; the fix is cheap enough to apply regardless.
